steps.title: Orient
steps.guidance: We’ll map the pelvic floor — the hammock of muscles that supports control. No action yet, just understanding.

steps.title: Picture it
steps.guidance: Imagine a sling running front to back between your sit bones. That sling is what we train.

steps.title: Why it matters
steps.guidance: A responsive pelvic floor means steadier control and stronger erections. Consistency beats intensity.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: Sit or lie comfortably. Breathe slowly and let your body soften.

steps.title: Locate
steps.guidance: Imagine stopping the flow of urine. The muscles that tighten are your target. Keep stomach and thighs relaxed.

steps.title: Gentle holds
steps.guidance: Squeeze for 3 seconds, release for 3. Breathe normally throughout.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: Comfortable position, slow breath.

steps.title: Lengthen
steps.guidance: On each exhale, gently push down and out — the opposite of a squeeze. This releases tension.

steps.title: Rest
steps.guidance: Let everything soften. Notice the difference between holding and releasing.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: Close your eyes if that feels right.

steps.title: Box breath
steps.guidance: Inhale 4, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4. Let the rhythm steady your nervous system.

steps.title: Return
steps.guidance: Let your breath return to normal. Notice the calm.

steps.title: Warm up
steps.guidance: A few slow breaths to begin.

steps.title: Combined sets
steps.guidance: Squeeze for 5, release fully for 5, then a gentle reverse push for 5. Repeat.

steps.title: Cool down
steps.guidance: Soften completely. Rest.

steps.title: Frame it
steps.guidance: Stop-start trains you to recognise and ride the point of no return. This is a mental rehearsal of that skill.

steps.title: Rise and pause
steps.guidance: Imagine arousal climbing. At a high point, pause and breathe until it settles. Repeat the rise and pause.

steps.title: Integrate
steps.guidance: Notice you can influence the wave. Control is a skill you’re building.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: Somewhere private and unhurried.

steps.title: Attend
steps.guidance: Touch your own skin slowly, with curiosity not goal. Notice sensation for its own sake.

steps.title: Close
steps.guidance: Rest. There was nothing to achieve — only to notice.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: If your partner is present, agree this is pressure-free. If not, rehearse it in your mind.

steps.title: Attend
steps.guidance: Slow, curious touch with no goal of performance. Focus on giving and receiving sensation.

steps.title: Close
steps.guidance: Rest together or reflect. Connection over outcome.

steps.title: Understand
steps.guidance: Overstimulation can dull arousal. This is about rebalancing, not shame.

steps.title: Reflect
steps.guidance: Bring to mind real intimacy — warmth, touch, presence. Let that be the cue, not a screen.

steps.title: Commit
steps.guidance: Choose one small change for today. Small and repeated wins.

steps.title: Warm up
steps.guidance: Slow breaths, gentle holds to begin.

steps.title: Functional holds
steps.guidance: Longer holds — 8 to 10 seconds — with full release between. Quality over count.

steps.title: Cool down
steps.guidance: Release and rest.

steps.title: Relax
steps.guidance: Settle into calm. Slow breath.

steps.title: Rehearse
steps.guidance: Picture a calm, confident encounter from start to finish. Feel steadiness, not pressure.

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: Carry that steady feeling with you. You’ve practised it.

steps.title: Why it helps
steps.guidance: Open talk lowers performance pressure more than any technique.

steps.title: Rehearse a line
steps.guidance: Plan one honest, kind sentence you could say to your partner about going slow. Practise it in your mind.

steps.title: Commit
steps.guidance: Decide when you might share it. No rush.

steps.title: Reframe
steps.guidance: A first or next encounter is an experience to share, not a test to pass.

steps.title: Rehearse calm
steps.guidance: Picture yourself relaxed, present, and unhurried. Breathe into that image.

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: Confidence is built in small reps like this one.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: Sit or lie down. Soften your jaw, shoulders, and belly. Nothing to achieve here.

steps.title: Find the release
steps.guidance: Instead of squeezing, gently let go — the feeling of releasing downward, like the start of a slow exhale. No force, just softening.

steps.title: Practice
steps.guidance: Breathe in slowly; on each exhale, let the pelvic floor drop and relax a little more. Squeezing builds strength — releasing builds control.

steps.title: Close
steps.guidance: A floor that can relax is as trained as one that can squeeze. Well done.

steps.title: Frame
steps.guidance: Arousal follows calm, not pressure. We're building ease in the body, not chasing a performance.

steps.title: Breathe into it
steps.guidance: Slow, low breaths. As the body settles, things have room to respond naturally. Nothing to push.

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: Recall a moment you felt easy and at home in your body. Let that steadiness be your baseline.

steps.title: Normalise
steps.guidance: A first time, or a return after a long gap, deserves patience. Nerves are normal — they are not a verdict on you.

steps.title: Rehearse calm
steps.guidance: Picture the moment unfolding slowly. Pauses are allowed. Breathing is allowed. Ease over urgency.

steps.title: One intention
steps.guidance: Set a single gentle intention: presence over performance.

steps.title: Notice
steps.guidance: Notice the pull toward a screen without judging it. Awareness is the first lever, not willpower.

steps.title: Redirect
steps.guidance: When the urge rises, breathe and bring to mind real connection — warmth, touch, presence. Let that be the cue.

steps.title: Small win
steps.guidance: Choose one screen-free choice today. Small, repeated wins rewire the pattern over time.

steps.title: Warm up
steps.guidance: A few gentle squeezes with full releases to wake the muscles. Relaxed stomach and thighs.

steps.title: Edge awareness
steps.guidance: Notice arousal building, and practise easing back before the peak — squeeze, breathe, soften. Control is a skill you build, not a switch you flip.

steps.title: Integrate
steps.guidance: Carry the same awareness into real moments: pause, breathe, continue.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: Sit tall but easy. Let the shoulders drop.

steps.title: Lengthen the out-breath
steps.guidance: Breathe in for a count of four, out for a count of six. The long exhale tells your body it is safe to relax.

steps.title: Rest
steps.guidance: Let breathing return to normal. Notice the calm you made.

steps.title: Connect
steps.guidance: Rest a hand low on your belly. Breathe slow and low, not high in the chest.

steps.title: Soften on the exhale
steps.guidance: As you breathe out, let the pelvic floor gently release downward. Breath and muscle, working together.

steps.title: Close
steps.guidance: No effort to hold anything. Just easy breath.

steps.title: Check in
steps.guidance: Hand on your belly and thigh — these should stay still and soft.

steps.title: Isolate
steps.guidance: Lift only the pelvic floor, without clenching your stomach, thighs, or buttocks. Smaller and cleaner beats bigger and tense.

steps.title: Release fully
steps.guidance: Let go completely between each lift. The release is half the work.

steps.title: Warm up
steps.guidance: A few short lifts and full releases to wake the muscles.

steps.title: Hold a little longer
steps.guidance: Lift gently and hold for a slow count of five, then release for five. Keep breathing the whole time.

steps.title: Ease off
steps.guidance: Shake out, relax. Consistency beats strain.

steps.title: Meet them
steps.guidance: Two muscles at the base — the ischiocavernosus and bulbocavernosus — do much of the control work. No need to memorise the names.

steps.title: What they do
steps.guidance: They squeeze at the base to support firmness and influence the timing of release. Training them is what these sessions build.

steps.title: Why it helps
steps.guidance: Responsive muscles mean steadier control. Gentle, regular practice is the path.

steps.title: Two directions
steps.guidance: A healthy pelvic floor does two things well: it lifts (squeeze) and it lets go (release).

steps.title: Balance matters
steps.guidance: All squeezing and no releasing leaves muscles tight and tired. We train both, on purpose.

steps.title: Takeaway
steps.guidance: Strength and relaxation together — that is control.

steps.title: Set up
steps.guidance: Lie back, knees bent, body soft.

steps.title: Release on the exhale
steps.guidance: Breathe in slowly; on the long exhale, let the pelvic floor open and drop, like an easing of tension. No pushing.

steps.title: Notice
steps.guidance: That downward softening is the reverse kegel. This is the skill that builds control.

steps.title: Scan
steps.guidance: Move attention from jaw to shoulders to belly to pelvic floor. Notice where you hold.

steps.title: Let each go
steps.guidance: At each spot, soften on an out-breath. Tension stored in the body works against you here.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: End loose and easy.

steps.title: Warm up
steps.guidance: A few easy lifts and releases.

steps.title: Alternate with rhythm
steps.guidance: Squeeze for two, release for two, in a smooth rhythm. Never hold tension at the bottom.

steps.title: Cool down
steps.guidance: Slow it right down, then stop. Relaxed and done.

steps.title: Coordinate
steps.guidance: Pair each lift with an out-breath, each release with an in-breath.

steps.title: Practise
steps.guidance: Move slowly so breath and muscle stay together. Control comes from coordination, not force.

steps.title: Finish
steps.guidance: Let everything settle.

steps.title: Frame
steps.guidance: Picture arousal as a ladder from one to ten. The skill is noticing the rungs.

steps.title: Ease at the edge
steps.guidance: As you sense the higher rungs, slow down, breathe, soften — let it settle a step before continuing.

steps.title: Learn
steps.guidance: Each time you ease back, you teach your body that you are in charge.

steps.title: Set up
steps.guidance: Get calm and unhurried.

steps.title: Insert the pause
steps.guidance: At the point of rising urgency, fully pause — three slow breaths — before going on. The pause is the practice.

steps.title: Carry it
steps.guidance: This same pause works in real moments.

steps.title: Notice the cue
steps.guidance: Spot what pulls you to a screen — boredom, stress, habit. Naming it loosens its grip.

steps.title: Swap the cue
steps.guidance: Decide one real alternative in advance: a walk, a call, a few breaths. Plan beats willpower.

steps.title: Commit
steps.guidance: Pick today's swap. One is enough.

steps.title: Watch it rise
steps.guidance: An urge is a wave — it builds, peaks, and falls if you let it.

steps.title: Ride, don't fight
steps.guidance: Breathe and observe the urge without acting. Notice it soften on its own.

steps.title: Note the win
steps.guidance: You did not have to obey it. That is the muscle you are building.

steps.title: Stand
steps.guidance: On your feet, relaxed.

steps.title: Engage while moving
steps.guidance: Do gentle lifts while standing, walking, or shifting weight — control you can use in real life, not just lying down.

steps.title: Release
steps.guidance: Soften fully at the end.

steps.title: Idea
steps.guidance: A light, well-timed squeeze before a moment of rising arousal can steady you.

steps.title: Practise the timing
steps.guidance: Rehearse a gentle lift, hold briefly, release — getting the timing smooth and unforced.

steps.title: Integrate
steps.guidance: Smooth, not forced. That is mastery.

steps.title: Picture it
steps.guidance: Imagine an intimate moment unfolding slowly and easily, with room to breathe.

steps.title: Stay with ease
steps.guidance: If tension creeps in, pause the scene, breathe, and restart calm. You are training the nervous system.

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: Calm rehearsed is calm available.

steps.title: Normalise
steps.guidance: Moments do not always go to plan. That is human, not failure.

steps.title: Rehearse the recovery
steps.guidance: Picture a stumble, then yourself staying warm and unbothered — a breath, a smile, carrying on. Recovery is a skill too.

steps.title: Close
steps.guidance: Kindness to yourself is part of the training.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: Somewhere private and unhurried. Slow your breathing first.

steps.title: Touch with the breath
steps.guidance: Move slow, curious touch in time with your breath. No goal but to notice what each spot feels like.

steps.title: Rest
steps.guidance: Nothing to achieve. Just what you noticed.

steps.title: Begin
steps.guidance: Sit or lie comfortably, unhurried.

steps.title: Explore beyond the obvious
steps.guidance: Bring attention to hands, forearms, chest, neck — neutral curiosity, no destination. Sensation for its own sake.

steps.title: Close
steps.guidance: Let the attention soften and rest.

steps.title: Agree
steps.guidance: Agree it is pressure-free. If your partner is not here, rehearse it in your mind.

steps.title: Give, then receive
steps.guidance: One gives slow, curious touch; the other simply receives. Then swap. No goal of performance.

steps.title: Reflect
steps.guidance: Connection over outcome. Notice how that felt.

steps.title: Set the tone
steps.guidance: Pressure-free, unhurried, together or rehearsed.

steps.title: Say it out loud
steps.guidance: As you give or receive touch, name simply what feels pleasant. Words build presence and lower pressure.

steps.title: Close
steps.guidance: Rest together. Closeness, not a test.

steps.title: Reframe
steps.guidance: Slowing down or pausing is always allowed — in rehearsal and in real life.

steps.title: Rehearse the pause
steps.guidance: Picture a moment, then yourself calmly slowing, breathing, continuing when ready. The pause is yours to take.

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: Knowing you can pause is what lets you relax.

steps.title: Reframe
steps.guidance: An encounter is shared experience, not a result to deliver.

steps.title: Rest attention on connection
steps.guidance: Picture focusing on warmth, touch, and the other person — not on monitoring yourself.

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: Presence is the whole skill. Build it in small reps like this.

steps.title: Why it helps
steps.guidance: Communication is as much hearing them as speaking. Curiosity lowers pressure for both.

steps.title: Rehearse a question
steps.guidance: Plan one kind, open question you could ask your partner about what they enjoy or want to slow down.

steps.title: Commit
steps.guidance: Decide a calm moment to ask. No rush.

steps.title: Idea
steps.guidance: A gentle word afterward builds trust as much as anything beforehand.

steps.title: Rehearse a line
steps.guidance: Plan one warm, honest sentence for after — appreciation or a small reflection, not analysis.

steps.title: Commit
steps.guidance: Kindness, on both sides of the moment.

steps.title: Set up
steps.guidance: Lie back, knees bent, fully soft.

steps.title: Exhale-led release
steps.guidance: On each long out-breath, let the pelvic floor drop and open a little more. Releasing, never pushing.

steps.title: Notice
steps.guidance: This softening is the skill that gives you control.

steps.title: Settle
steps.guidance: Slow the breath; soften jaw and shoulders.

steps.title: Let go, calmly
steps.guidance: Picture an easy, unhurried moment as you release the pelvic floor on each exhale. Calm body, open muscles.

steps.title: Close
steps.guidance: Loose and easy. Well done.

steps.title: Relax in order
steps.guidance: Soften feet, legs, belly, chest, shoulders, jaw — one by one. A calm body is the foundation.

steps.title: Let it follow
steps.guidance: With the body at ease, things respond in their own time. Nothing to force or chase.

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: Ease first. The rest follows.

steps.title: Frame
steps.guidance: Pressure works against you here. Patience works with you.

steps.title: Breathe and wait
steps.guidance: Slow breaths, no monitoring, no rushing. Let things build at their own pace.

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: Trust, not effort.

steps.title: Reframe
steps.guidance: Readiness is built in small, low-pressure reps — not one big leap.

steps.title: Picture one step
steps.guidance: Imagine a single, easy, low-stakes step forward and yourself calm through it.

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: One small step counts. You've taken one now.

steps.title: Notice the voice
steps.guidance: Spot the harsh inner line — 'what if I fail'. Naming it loosens it.

steps.title: Replace it
steps.guidance: Swap in something steady and true: 'I can go slow, I can pause, I am okay.'

steps.title: Anchor
steps.guidance: How you speak to yourself shapes how you show up.

steps.title: Sit with it
steps.guidance: Let a dull, restless moment exist without reaching for a screen. It passes.

steps.title: Breathe through
steps.guidance: Three slow breaths. Notice the restlessness rise and fade on its own.

steps.title: Note the win
steps.guidance: You did not need the screen. That is the rewire.

steps.title: Spot the reward
steps.guidance: Screens offer a fast, hollow reward. Real life offers slower, fuller ones.

steps.title: Choose one
steps.guidance: Pick one real reward for today — movement, a call, sunlight, rest — and plan when.

steps.title: Commit
steps.guidance: One real choice, repeated, retrains the pull.

steps.title: Warm up
steps.guidance: A few gentle squeezes with full releases.

steps.title: Ease with the exhale
steps.guidance: As arousal rises, use a long, slow out-breath to ease it back a step. Breath is your steadiest brake.

steps.title: Integrate
steps.guidance: Slow exhale, then continue. Usable anywhere.

steps.title: Map it
steps.guidance: Learn your own cues for rising arousal — the early rungs, not just the top.

steps.title: Ease earlier
steps.guidance: Practise easing back at an early rung — squeeze, breathe, soften — before urgency peaks. Earlier is easier.

steps.title: Integrate
steps.guidance: The sooner you notice, the more control you have.
